sections.heading: Overview
sections.content:
> **DEPRECATED:** The test runner and test dashboard execution/results features are deprecated. The viz test tab is being simplified to test discovery and annotation visibility only — showing test functions, their tier classification, purpose strings, step narratives, and validates edges. Pass/fail badges, JUnit ingestion, the Run Tests button, and test run history are being removed. Test execution belongs in the terminal.

The cortex test runner executes pytest and records results in the index database, mapping each test to the production code it validates. This enables scoped test runs ("run only the tests that cover this function") and test status in the viz dashboard.

sections.heading: Quick Start
sections.content:
```bash
# Run all tests and record results
cortex test .

# Run only tests that validate a specific function
cortex test . --node myproject::pm.database::get_engine

# Run tests covering all code under a path
cortex test . --path pm/pipeline/

# Pass extra pytest flags
cortex test . -v --tb=short

# Dry run without saving to DB
cortex test . --no-save
```

sections.heading: Core Functionality
sections.content:
- **Scoped runs:** `--node` finds all tests with `validates` edges to that node. `--path` finds all production nodes under that path and their validators.
- **Auto-detect runner:** Tries pixi, poetry, then plain pytest automatically. Override with `--runner`.
- **Result mapping:** Each pytest test ID is resolved to a cortex node ID via `validates` edges. Unmapped tests are flagged.
- **Persistent results:** `test_runs` table stores run metadata; `test_results` stores per-test status. The viz dashboard reads these for the test panel.
- **Git SHA tracking:** Each run records the current git commit for traceability.

sections.heading: Configuration & Options
sections.content:
| Option | Description | Default |
|---|---|---|
| `--node <id>` | Scope to tests validating this node | All tests |
| `--path <prefix>` | Scope to tests covering nodes under this path | All tests |
| `--no-save` | Don't write results to cortex.db | Save |
| `--runner <cmd>` | Override runner (e.g. `pixi run pytest`) | Auto-detect |
| `PYTEST_ARGS...` | Extra args passed through to pytest | None |

For best results, install `pytest-json-report` in your project for richer test data.

sections.heading: Best Practices & Troubleshooting
sections.content:
### Best Practices
- Connect tests to production code with `cortex link <test_node> --edge-type validates --to <prod_node> .`
- Use `@test_workflow(covers=[...])` dFlow decorators — cortex build auto-creates validates edges from these
- Run `cortex test .` in CI to maintain a history of test results per commit

### Common Issues
- **Problem:** Many unmapped tests in output
- **Solution:** Add validates edges: `cortex link <test_id> --edge-type validates --to <prod_id> .`

- **Problem:** `cortex test . --node X` runs no tests
- **Solution:** No validates edges point to node X. Check with `cortex graph X . --direction in`
